engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is deciding whether to trust licensed professionals with their health; the page sells defined expertise (Registered Dietitians), leads with credentials, team bios, process and testimonials/reviews. That is the top rung of the ladder.

goal.one_action: Book a free phone/consultation call with a dietitian

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page trust site: home overview + services + about/team + contact, with a persistent free-consult booking CTA and newsletter capture in the footer. Nav lists 6 items (Nutrition Videos not crawled).

mapped.observed_as: top nav (About Us, Services, Health Professionals, Speaking, Nutrition Videos, Contact)
Licensed Dietitians Victoria BC & Nanaimo / Online Nutrition Support Across British Columbia
Ready to get personalized nutrition support from a registered dietitian?
How Our Dietitians Support Your Nutrition Goals (service category cards: adults, sports, kids, workplaces)
Adult / Family & Child / Sports nutrition service descriptions
What People Are Saying About Our Dietitians
Leave a Review / Google reviews widget
Meet Our Licensed Dietitians / Meet the Dietitian Team
Frequently Asked Questions About Working with a Dietitian; What is the difference between a dietitian and a nutritionist?
Nutritional Insight Session / service offering descriptions (no prices shown)
Where we're located / Nutrition Services in Victoria, Nanaimo & Across BC (service area + office map + hours)
Not sure which service is for you? Book an information call (free)
Kristen Yarker Dietitians Agency footer brand block + social links

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full; clear trust-engine service business with credentials, team, reviews, FAQ; single conversion action (book free consult via Calendly).

gap_findings: Persistent free-consult Calendly CTA is a single-step calendar embed (delegated form/redirect per the coltivar decision) — no on-site inventory/multi-step, so not a proposed capability; noted so it is not mistaken for a booking-* engine.
Multiple secondary audiences in nav (Health Professionals, Speaking) that a single conversion-action model does not express, but they fall outside the crawled/primary path.
